info.title: Logi Composer Rest API
info.description: The Composer REST API provides methods for retrieving, updating, and deleting Composer metadata. With the API endpoints, you can manage connections, data sources, accounts, groups, and users quickly and easily.

get.summary: Returns a list of the sources
get.description: Requires READ permission on the sources

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: Defines filter to search by. Supports only boolean, string, number, date time literal types on primary fields like name, id, description etc. Operators supported are =, != or <>, like, ilike, >, >=, <, <=, is null, is not null,  in, and, or, not, is empty, is not empty. Tag supports in, and, is empty, is not empty, not operators. Other operators might not return expected result. Nested objects, array properties are not supported.

parameters.name: sort

Each of the data entity types corresponds to it’s own definition block:

  • SINGLE_COLLECTION - singleCollection
  • CUSTOM_SQL - customSql
  • INDEX - index
  • UPLOAD - upload

nativeFields.description:
This field is optional and will be calculated automatically based on the table if missing.
NOTE: List of native fields is not available for the listing endpoint

forbiddenFeatures.description: This field is optional and will be calculated automatically based on the table if missing.

FieldFormatResource.description:
Field format. Each of the format blocks applicable only for certain data types:

  • time - TIME
  • number - NUMBER

There is no format for ATTRIBUTE, TEXT and HIERARCHY fields

FieldOriginResource.description:
Field origin. Each of the origin types corresponds to it’s own field definition block:

  • NATIVE - nativeOrigin
  • DERIVED - derivedOrigin
  • HIERARCHY - hierarchyOrigin

nativeOrigin.description: Defines a native field that is stored in the database. It’s required if origin.type = NATIVE

derivedOrigin.description: Defines derived field that can be calculated based on other fields. It’s required if origin.type = DERIVED

hierarchyOrigin.description: Defines the hierarchy field. It’s required if origin.type = HIERARCHY

labelField.description: Field that contains label to display on the visualization instead of hierarchical value

Index.description: Represents Elasticsearch target for the query. It can be single index, multiple indexes or pattern

connectionId.description: Connection id. Must be present in the system

pattern.description:
Pattern used to match collections in the database. Pattern syntax depends on patternType:

  • NATIVE - supports wildcard (*). Example: log-*-*
  • TIME_BASED - supports java time format. Example: log-yyyy-MM-dd

dataEntityId.description: Defines the data entity to be joined

dimension.description: Complex sources could join many data entities, where some joined data are present only to potentially enrich results with some additional information. If that information is not required by the query results, we get no benefits from additional JOINs but pay price in the form of additional operations. The dimension flag allows dropping unused joined data entities and improves performance considerably. NOTE: Setting this flag to true may lead to incorrect data result if this data entity is not a dimension

override.description: Statistics override. By default statistics are loaded from the data source and cached in QE

If you want to disable/enable automatic refresh for this field, you may use this flag. It doesn't have an effect if cache is disabled or override is defined
